QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_48

question.text: What is the difference between 'initial margin' and 'maintenance margin' in futures trading?

answer.text: Initial margin is the upfront deposit required to open a position; maintenance margin is the minimum level below which the account cannot fall — if it does, a margin call requires the trader to restore it to initial margin level
Initial margin is higher and is paid once; maintenance margin is a daily fee for holding the position
Initial margin is for long positions; maintenance margin is for short positions
They are the same — both refer to the minimum required deposit

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Initial margin: deposited when opening a position (typically the SPAN margin). Maintenance margin: the threshold (usually 75-80% of initial) below which the account triggers a margin call. If daily MTM losses erode the account below maintenance, the trader must bring it back to the initial margin level. **The Trap:** Maintenance margin is not a fee — it's a trigger level. Both apply to long AND short positions. They are different amounts (initial > maintenance). **Cross-Exam Context:** This margin structure is standard across all exchange-traded futures markets — equity (VIII), currency (I), IR (IV), commodity (XVI). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always maintain margin well above the maintenance level — a margin call during volatile markets can force premature position exit at unfavorable prices.
